all_candidates.title: Tour de France 2017: Stage 18 Recap | NBC Sports

transcript.clean_text: >> THE BREAKAWAY THAT TRAVELED TOGETHER FOR SO MUCH OF STAGE 19 DIDN'T STAND A CHANCE AT THE END. EDVALD BOASSON HAGEN BOLTS AWAY AND ENJOYS THE FINISHING SCENE TO HIMSELF. HE WINS A STAGE FOR THE FIRST TIME IN THIS YEAR'S TOUR de FRANCE AND THE THIRD TIME OF HIS CAREER. TEAM SKY AND THE LEADER, THE OVERALL LEADER, CHRIS FROOME, TUCKED IN THERE IN THE PELOTON, ABOUT FOUR MINUTES AWAY FROM FINISHING. FROOME NO DANGER TODAY, WILL STAY IN YELLOW TO STAGE 20 TOMORROW. WHAT WENT SO RIGHT FOR BOASSON HAGEN AT THE END. >> Robbie: I THINK WHAT HE HAD WAS THE SURPLUS OF POWER AND LUXURY, KNOWING HE'S THE FASTEST MAN OUT OF THE RIDERS, SO HE HAD THE LUXURY OF FOLLOWING THE WHEELS, SO STRONG, SO CLASSY, SUCH A GREAT BIKE RACER, SO CLOSE ON MANY STAGES AND GOT THE STAGE WIN. THAT'S A PRETTY GOOD PLAN B. >> WHEN MARK CAVENDISH CRASHED OUT EARLY IN THE TOUR. >> GREAT PLAN B. I LOVE PLAN B. WE SAW THE COURSE THIS MORNING. WE SAW THE ROUNDABOUT, WE SAID THIS IS OBVIOUSLY A RIGHT ROUNDABOUT, WHERE YOU COULD GO EITHER WAY. THERE WASN'T A WRONG WAY, BUT NOT A GOOD WAY. >> EXACTLY. SO OBVIOUSLY, YOU HAD TO GO RIGHT THERE. EDVALD BOASSON HAGEN WENT RIGHT ALONG WITH ARNDT AND THOSE GUYS HAD A 50-METER ADVANTAGE GOING THROUGH THERE. HE SAW HE HAD A GAP AND HIS TOWN OF SPEED IS SO HIGH. HE HAD MORE SPEED, MORE OR LESS THAN EVERYONE IN THE PELOTON, WAS MISSING THIS OR THAT, ARE COMING TO THE LINE A LITTLE TOO LATE, SO YEAH, HE HAD SO MUCH CLASS. I CAN'T SAY ENOUGH ABOUT THE GUY, ON AND OFF THE BIKE. GREAT TEAMMATE TO HAVE A MARK CAVENDISH BEING HERE, BUT FOR THE TEAM TO HAVE MARK CAVENDISH BEING OUT OF THE RACE AND NOT HAVING THE STAGE LIKE SO MANY OTHER TEAMS, TO GET THAT SO CLOSE TO PARIS, YOU SAW DOUGLAS RIDER, THE PRINCIPAL, SO THANKFUL. >> A RIDER WHO WENTS FOR THE FIRST TIME, BACKING UP TO EARLIER IN THIS TOUR, HE CAME SO CLOSE. HE INTRODUCED THE TERM, 3/10,000 OF A SECOND, AS HE BARELY LOST TO MARCEL KITTEL IN STAGE 7. >> >> Robbie: THIS IS AN INDICATION OF HOW GOOD HE IS PRINTING, AGAINST MARK CAVENDISH. THAT IS, IN MY ESTIMATION, TOO CLOSE TO CALL AND IF THE CRAM IS A MICRON ON ONE SIDE OR THE OTHER AND IF IT WAS THE OTHER SIDE OF THE STREET, BOASSON HAGEN MIGHT HAVE GOTTEN THAT. I CALL THAT ONE WIN, ONE TIE. >> MAYBE HE REMEMBERED THAT ALL TOO WELL. HE WASN'T GOING TO LET IT BE NEARLY THAT CLOSE AT THE 2KM MARK. >> HE'S IN THE MIDDLE OF THE SCREEN THERE. HE HITS THE AFTER BURNERS AND GOES RIGHT ACROSS THE GAP WHEN MICHAEL ALBASINI IS PUTTING EVERYTHING IN, ALBASINI CAN'T BELIEVE WHAT HE'S SEEING. BOASSON HAGEN GOES AGAIN AND AGAIN. THEY WERE ABLE TO CHASE HIM DOWN. ALBASINI WITH HIS DIRECT EXCEPTOR, CHAVANEL ON HIS WHEEL, GENDT MADE A GO OF IT. BOASSON HAGEN CAN COVER ALL OF THE GAPS. >> THESE GUYS WEREN'T BORN YESTERDAY. THEY KNOW THAT BOASSON HAGEN IS EASILY THE FASTEST AND STRONGEST BUY ON THE BREAKAWAY, SO THEY KNEW THEY HAD TO MARK ONE PERSON. THAT RIGHT-HAND TURN, AROUND THE ROUNDABOUT, LOOK AT THE GAP THAT THEY WENT THROUGH THERE, SO MUCH FASTER, AND IT WASN'T THAT IT WAS THAT MUCH SHORTER THROUGH THE LEFT-HAND SIDE OR THE RIGHT-HAND SIDE, BUT YOU DIDN'T HAVE TO BREAK AT ALL. THOSE GUYS HAD TO BREAK, SLOW DOWN AND REACCELERATE, OBVIOUSLY, THERE'S NO WAY, BUT LOOK AT THIS, HIS ACCELERATION, HE JUST DROVE, RIGHT OFF THE WHEEL. >> NICHOLAS ARNDT TRYING TO FIND THE WHEEL, CAN'T IMAGINE, WATCHING THE STAGE WIN VANISH, WHAT'S GOING THROUGH HIS HEAD, THIS CAN'T BE HAPPENING. >> IT WAS ALMOST AS IF HE WAS MOTOR PACING AND COULDN'T STAY ON THE MOTOR OF EDVALD BOASSON HAGEN AND THIS IS THE ONLY MOMENT THAT COULD HAVE GONE WRONG AND EDVALD BOASSON HAGEN WAS ABLE TO KEEP IT UP, I'M SURE HIS TEAM MANAGEMENT WAS SO THANKFUL. A CLEAN SET OF WHEELS. A GREAT VICTORY. A HELPING HAND. THAT'S THEIR SYMBOL ON THE BACK OF THEIR JERSEY, HISTORICALLY, SO GREAT WIN. >> SALUTE. >> THAT LAST LOOK BACK HAD TO FEEL SO GOOD WHEN HE REALIZED HE WAS THAT FAR OUT IN FRONT, CELEBRATION AFTERWARD. BOASSON HAGEN FIRST WIN INDIVIDUALLY IN THE 2017 TOUR. SO A COUPLE MOMENTS AGO, WE SHOWED THIS, THERE'S TEAM SKY COMING IN, AND RIGHT THIS SAFELY WITH THEM IS CHRIS FROOME, THE OVERALL LEADERS. IN TERMS OF HISTORY, IT'S THE SMALLEST CUSHION ESPECIALLY WHETHER YOU LOOK AT THE TOP FOUR. IT'S GOING TO BE A FANTASTIC TIME TRIAL TOMORROW. THERE'S NOT MUCH MOVEMENT, SIX SECONDS BEN BARDET AND HERON. >> IF HE DOESN'T CRASH. IF HE'S HIS NORMAL TIME TRIALING SELF, HOW MUCH BETTER IS HE THAN HIS COMPETITORS. >> 45 SECONDS AND MAYBE OVER A MINUTE BETTER IN THE CIRCUMSTANCES WE'LL SEE TOMORROW, BARRING CATASTROPHE. >> BACK BOOST TO EDVALD BOASSON HAGEN AND -- BACK TO BOOST AND HIS STAGE WIN TODAY. EVERYBODY KNOWS HE'S THE FASTEST, IS THERE ANYTHING THEY COULD HAVE DONE TO PREVENT HIM? >> TAKE A SHORT CUT, WHICH HE ALREADY DID. >> THE WRONG WAY. >> EXACTLY. HE GOT LOST. >> THERE WASN'T ANYTHING. YOU SAW THE GUYS STILL ATTACKING HIM. YOU ASKED WHAT ARE THEY GOING TO DO? THEY'LL ATTACK HIM. THEY ATTACKED HIM, DID EVERYTHING THEY COULD BUT YOU SAW HIM AN POINTED OUT HOW FAST, HE WOULD JUST SHUT EVERY ONE OF THOSE ATTACKS DOWN HIMSELF. IT WASN'T LIKE HE HAD A TEAMMATE CLOSING THE GAPS DOWN FOR HIM. HE WAS DOING IT HIMSELF. TOOK TWO OR THREE PEDAL STROKES, EASE LIT STRONGEST, FASTEST GUY. DESERVED WINNER. >> SECOND PLACE, FIVE SECONDS BEHIND, NICHOLAS ARNDT, SHARED HIS THOUGHTS. >> BOASSON HAGEN IS SUCH A GOOD SPRINTER. HOW SURPRISED WERE YOU THAT HE ATTACKED FROM DISTANCE? >> NOT AT YOU WILL. HE WAS FOLLOWING ATTACKS AND YOU -- NOT AT ALL. HE WAS FOLLOWING ATTACKS AND IT WOULDN'T COME TO A BUNCH SPRINT, NO ONE WAS ATTACKING, NO ONE WANTED TO GO WITH BOASSON HAGEN OR ME TO THE FINISH LINE, BECAUSE WE HAD THE TWO FASTEST SPRINTERS I THINK IN THIS GROUP, SO ONCE WE GET TO ATTACKING, HE ATTACKED WITH ME IN THE ROUNDABOUT AT 3K TO GO AND I ACTUALLY WENT IN FRONT, HE WAS ON MY WHEEL, STRAIGHT OUT OF THE ROUNDABOUT, HE WENT FULL GAS AND I COULDN'T HOLD THE WHEEL. I WANTED SURPRISED BUT IT WAS SO STRONG FOR HIM, I COULDN'T HOLD IT. >> DID SPEED. YOU HEARD ABOUT LEADING UP TO THAT POINT, SEPARATES FROM THE BREAKAWAY, WINS FOR THE FIRST TIME IN THIS TOUR DE FRANCE, AFTER COMING WITHIN MILLIMETERS, THANKS FOR THE FLYOVER AND DEATH SPIRAL. >> ANNOUNCING BOASSON HAGEN'S ARRIVAL. >> THERE'S LOOK ON HIS FACE AFTER HIS FIRST WIN. ON THE SCREEN, BOOST FIVE SECONDS BETTER THAN ARNDT. >> AND 17 SECONDS FROM THE CHASE GROUP. SO IMPRESSIVE IN THE LAST COUPLE OF KILOMETERS, AGAINST KEUKELEIRE. BENNATI. GREAT RIDING BY DE GENDT. EDVALD BOASSON HAGEN WAS NOT TO BE DENIED. >> FINALLY YOU HAVE A VICTORY, AND YOU MUST BE DELIGHTED. >> I'M REALLY HAPPY. THE TEAM DID WELL BEFORE KNEE. ON THE CLIMB, CAME DOWN, ON THE BREAKAWAY, AND WE WERE TOGETHER THE WHOLE DAY. I MADE A FINAL ATTACK, AND NOBODY CAN FOLLOW. I'M REALLY HAPPY. >> YOU COULD HAVE WAITED FOR THE SPRINTS, BUT DECIDED TO ANTICIPATE? >> YEAH, I COULD HAVE WAITED, BUT I ALSO WAS FEELING QUITE GOOD. BIG GAP, AND FINALLY GET ACROSS THE LINE ALONE. I'M REALLY HAPPY. >> CONGRATULATIONS. >> EDVALD BOASSON HAGEN WINS STAGE 19, THE YELLOW JERSEY, EVEN THE LONGEST STAGE OF THIS YEAR'S TOUR, 138 MILES. DIDN'T EVEN MAKE A BIT OF DIFFERENCE AT THE TOP. LOSING THE SPRINT STAGE, TOOK THE TEAM A WHILE TO REGROUP. AND LOSE BY SO LITTLE, HAVE THE DETERMINATION, TO COME BACK AND KEEP TRYING, EDVALD BOASSON HAGEN, AN IMPRESSIVE TOUR DE FRANCE THIS YEAR. >> HE CELEBRATES HIS STAGE WIN. COMI
